skill.name: qwen-image-pro
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI, with guidance for text-heavy posters, banners, and reference-image workflows.
skill.summary: Use Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through inference.sh for image generation, editing, and text-focused visual design.
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline backticks, table text, and example prompts. The confirmed risk is that the skill intentionally enables the external infsh CLI and sends prompts, reference image URLs, and generated outputs through a third-party network service. No prompt injection attempt, malware behavior, credential harvesting, filesystem access, or real weak cryptographic algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External Image Generation CLI Sends Data to Third-Party Service
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed around inference.sh commands that submit prompts, reference image URLs, and generation settings to Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the infsh CLI. This is expected functionality, but users could disclose sensitive text or private image URLs to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation repeatedly instructs users to run infsh commands that perform remote image generation. The risk is data disclosure to the intended provider, not evidence of malicious exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences and inline backtick examples as Ruby or shell execution. Review found these are documentation examples rather than executable skill code, although they do demonstrate user-invoked infsh and npx commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown code blocks or inline documentation. There is no script file or automatic execution path in the reviewed skill content.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but the cited lines contain prose, model names, prompt examples, or table headings. No hash, cipher, signing, password storage, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operations at the reported locations. The findings appear to be keyword collisions in ordinary documentation text.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Example References
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh documentation, a hosted sample image, and example.com placeholder reference images. They are not hidden endpoints and do not include evidence of credential collection or covert data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL destinations are visible documentation, media, and placeholder references. Network use remains a normal operational requirement for this image generation skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Invoked External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill teaches users to run infsh app commands. This is permitted by the skill and required for functionality, but marketplace users should understand that prompts and image references leave the local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command pattern is explicit and central to the skill. The context supports a legitimate design workflow, not confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Text-heavy image generation often needs precise prompts, size settings, and negative prompts. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro workflows through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to generate images with alibaba/qwen-image-2-pro through infsh commands.
Provides prompt patterns for posters, banners, logos, and product images.
Documents reference image editing with one to three image URIs.
Explains generation controls such as width, height, seed, num_images, and watermark.
Includes text rendering guidance using quoted text and negative prompts.
Includes a Python SDK example for batch or streaming workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI or SDK and a valid service account.
Prompts and reference image URLs are sent to an external image service.
Does not provide local image generation or offline model weights.
Image quality and text accuracy still depend on prompt clarity and model behavior.

use_cases.title: Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Create banners and sale graphics that need readable promotional text and controlled dimensions.

use_cases.title: Concept Exploration
use_cases.description: Generate several visual directions from one prompt, then compare styles before commissioning final assets.

use_cases.title: Reference-Based Editing
use_cases.description: Use reference image URLs to guide edits, style transfer, or composition experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro to create one image of [subject] in [style]. Use [width] by [height] dimensions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Text Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro to create a poster with the exact title "[title]" and subtitle "[subtitle]". Set prompt_extend to false and include a negative prompt for blurry or distorted text.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Variations
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro to generate [number] variations of [asset type] for [brand or project]. Keep the visual style consistent and use seed [seed] if reproducibility matters.

prompt_templates.title: Edit with References
prompt_templates.prompt: Use qwen-image-pro with these reference image URLs: [url 1], [url 2]. Apply [specific edit] while preserving [identity, layout, or style constraint].

output_examples.input: A request for a summer sale banner with exact promotional text and wide dimensions.
output_examples.output: A ready-to-run infsh workflow with the model name, prompt wording, dimensions, prompt extension disabled, and a negative prompt for text clarity.

output_examples.input: A request to create four logo concepts for a coffee shop.
output_examples.output: A variation-focused generation plan that sets the number of images and keeps the brand name clear in the prompt.

output_examples.input: A request to edit a person image using an outfit reference.
output_examples.output: A reference-image editing workflow that describes the image roles and submits both source URLs to the model.

best_practices: Put exact visible text in quotation marks and describe its position, color, and font style.
Set prompt_extend to false when exact text or layout must be preserved.
Avoid private prompts, confidential branding, and nonpublic image URLs unless approved for third-party processing.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts when the output needs readable text or strict composition.
Do not submit sensitive reference images without confirming service and data policies.
Do not request dimensions outside the documented pixel constraints.

faq.question: What does this skill use to generate images?
faq.answer: It uses Alibaba Qwen-Image-2.0-Pro through the inference.sh CLI or Python SDK.

faq.question: Can it generate posters with readable text?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill focuses on text-heavy prompts and recommends exact quoted text, layout details, and negative prompts.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It requires inference.sh access and sends generation requests to an external service.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents reference image workflows using one to three image URLs.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What security caution should users know?
faq.answer: Prompts and image references may contain sensitive data, so review them before sending requests to inference.sh.
